page: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2702.69 and a strong PassMark memory score of 2924.1. Multi-core performance metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests yield average results, reaching 116.36 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 56.81 GB/sec for uncached large block writes. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the weak tier at 6.57 tokens/sec, whereas text generation for a small 135M model is strong at 311.93 tokens/sec.

The B32als_v2 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that experience variable CPU utilization due to its burstable allocation model. However, the burstable nature introduces performance variability if CPU credits are exhausted. The low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage are key tradeoffs, requiring external premium storage for data-intensive applications. This server is configured for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and light database operations that require high vCPU counts but have low memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. Operating at 3.525 GHz, it features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU and memory performance, while multi-core operations, web serving, and medium-model LLM inference perform in the average tier. Large-model LLM inference is weak. The burstable CPU allocation provides qualitative cost efficiency for variable workloads, though performance is subject to credit limits. This configuration is suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and low-memory database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable Basv2 virtual machine with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance and supports premium storage with no local disks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32als_v2 is a burstable Basv2 virtual machine featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and premium storage capability with no local disks.
tagline: A burstable 32-vCPU AMD EPYC instance with low memory density, designed for variable workloads requiring high core counts.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.
Features burstable CPU allocation, suitable for variable or intermittent workloads.
Lacks local storage but supports premium external storage options.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance with average multi-core throughput.
Best suited for software compilation, static web serving, and low-memory database tasks.
